title: Optimize Render Batching
description:
Fix a component that triggers multiple unnecessary re-renders by consolidating state updates and optimizing callback patterns.

### Requirements

- The `Dashboard` has three independent sections that currently re-render together — isolate them
- Extract each section into its own memoized component with minimal props
- Use `useCallback` for all handlers passed to child components
- Consolidate multiple related `setState` calls into a single state update using `useReducer` or batching
- Track render counts per section to verify isolation
- The "Add Item" action updates both the list and the counter — this should be a single render

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Add button | `add-btn` |
| Clear button | `clear-btn` |
| Filter input | `filter-input` |
| Item list | `item-list` |
| Item count | `item-count` |
| Filter section renders | `filter-renders` |
| List section renders | `list-renders` |
| Counter section renders | `counter-renders` |
constraints:
- Each section must be a separate memoized component
- Handlers must use useCallback
- Adding an item must NOT cause the filter section to re-render
- Filtering must NOT cause the counter section to re-render
- Render counts must accurately track actual renders
hints: React.memo on child components prevents re-render when props haven't changed. But if you pass a new function reference each render, memo won't help — use useCallback.
Multiple setState calls in an event handler are already batched in React 18. But if you have related state, useReducer is cleaner.
Put the filter input state inside the FilterSection component itself, so changes to it don't affect the parent.
